Update tests/unit/csp.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Claude/opencode hooks plugin support (#31)

* feat: add OpenCode plugin support

Adds a native TypeScript plugin (plugin/opencode.ts) that integrates
otel-hook with OpenCode's plugin system, following the same thin-wrapper
pattern as rtk.

- plugin/opencode.ts: exports OtelHookPlugin; checks otel-hook is in
  PATH at load time, then hooks session.created/deleted → SessionStart/
  SessionEnd and tool.execute.before/after → PreToolUse/PostToolUse,
  piping JSON payloads to otel-hook via stdin with IDE_OTEL_IDE_NAME=opencode

- setup.sh: adds --opencode flag and setup_opencode() function that
  copies the plugin to .opencode/plugins/ (project) or
  ~/.config/opencode/plugins/ (--global); auto-detects OpenCode via
  command -v opencode or ~/.config/opencode; respects OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR

- examples/opencode-plugin.example.ts: annotated copy for users who
  want to customise the plugin

- README.md: replaces the placeholder OpenCode blurb with a proper
  setup section including quick-start commands and manual install steps

- tests/test_otel_hook.py: adds TestOpenCodePluginPayloads (11 tests)
  covering IDE detection, event normalisation, and priority ordering for
  all four payload types emitted by the plugin

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* feat(opencode): handle all observable hook events

Extends plugin/opencode.ts (and the matching example) to cover the full
set of OpenCode events that map to canonical otel-hook events:

  session.idle    → Stop            (agent finished responding)
  message.updated → UserPromptSubmit (role=user messages only)
  session.error   → SessionEnd with status=error (closes trace on failure)

The previous commit only wired session.created/deleted and the tool
execute lifecycle. The three new branches follow the same thin-wrapper
pattern: build a minimal JSON payload and pipe it to otel-hook via
IDE_OTEL_IDE_NAME=opencode.

Tests: adds 8 new assertions to TestOpenCodePluginPayloads covering
IDE detection and event-name extraction for every payload shape the
updated plugin emits. All 185 tests pass.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* docs: update Supported Events table with OpenCode plugin column

- Splits "Claude Code / Antigravity / compatible runners" into two
  separate columns: one for Claude Code / Antigravity, one for
  OpenCode (plugin) showing the native OpenCode event names that
  each canonical event maps from (session.created, session.idle,
  message.updated, tool.execute.before/after, etc.)
- Updates the "Events captured" blurb under the OpenCode setup
  section to reflect the full list (UserPromptSubmit and Stop were
  missing) and adds a note about the upstream MCP tool call limitation

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* feat(setup): add --cursor --global support

setup.sh --cursor always wrote to the project-level .cursor/hooks.json;
there was no way to target the global ~/.cursor/hooks.json without
manually editing the file.

Changes:
- Add CURSOR_GLOBAL variable; --global now sets it alongside
  CLAUDE_GLOBAL and OPENCODE_GLOBAL
- setup_cursor() branches on CURSOR_GLOBAL: global path is
  ~/.cursor/hooks.json, project path is unchanged
- Echo label updated to show (global: ...) or (project: ...) like
  the Claude and OpenCode functions already do
- Next-steps output notes when global hooks.json was written
- Usage comment and README Quick Start updated with --cursor --global

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* feat(opencode): add AfterFileEdit and PostToolUseFailure; fix table

Source-verified against sst/opencode (dev branch) to close the gaps
in the event coverage:

plugin/opencode.ts + examples/opencode-plugin.example.ts:
- file.edited → AfterFileEdit: properties contain exactly {file:string}
  (no session_id); emitted by write/edit/apply_patch tools
- tool.execute.after now inspects output.metadata.exit (the only
  failure signal for bash); non-zero exit emits PostToolUseFailure
  with exit_code and error="exit <n>", zero/absent emits PostToolUse

README Supported Events table:
- PostToolUseFailure: tool.execute.after (exit≠0)
- AfterFileEdit: file.edited
- Added footnotes explaining that bash/read/write/MCP/subagent tool
  calls all route through tool.execute.before/after (→ PreToolUse/
  PostToolUse with the relevant tool_name)
- Removed incorrect MCP limitation note — sst/opencode wraps MCP
  calls in the same tool.execute.before/after hooks
- Updated "Events captured" blurb to list all 8 emitted event types

tests/test_otel_hook.py: 6 new assertions (25 total in
TestOpenCodePluginPayloads) covering AfterFileEdit and
PostToolUseFailure IDE detection, canonical-name check, and
_get_event_name round-trip.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HpMgSN8uCgNypFeuDCo7x3

* Update README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: PRB auto-strip screen-incompatible fields and retry

When Jira rejects fields with 'not on the appropriate screen', automatically
strip those fields and retry the create — fully transparent to the user.

On success, shows which fields were auto-removed (e.g. customfield_10265,
customfield_10254 on Defect; customfield_10253 on Story).
On retry failure, debugBlock shows the stripped payload actually sent.

commit.message:
Deploy presentation to GitHub Pages on merge to main (#9)

Adds a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes the Vite/React
presentation to GitHub Pages on every push to `main`.

## Changes Made

- **`.github/workflows/deploy-presentation.yml`**: New workflow with two
jobs:
- **build**: Checks out the code, installs dependencies via Bun, runs
the existing `bun run build` script with `--base=/${{
github.event.repository.name }}/` to ensure asset paths resolve
correctly under the GitHub Pages subpath, and uploads
`presentation/dist` as a Pages artifact. Has `contents: read` permission
only.
- **deploy**: Publishes the artifact to GitHub Pages via OIDC (no stored
credentials needed). Has `pages: write`, `id-token: write`, and
`contents: read` permissions.

Permissions are scoped per-job for least privilege — the `build` job
does not receive elevated Pages or OIDC permissions.

The Vite base path is derived from `${{ github.event.repository.name }}`
so the workflow remains portable across forks and renames.

## Action Versions Used

| Action | Version |
|---|---|
| `actions/checkout` | `v4` |
| `oven-sh/setup-bun` | `v2` |
| `actions/configure-pages` | `v5` |
| `actions/upload-pages-artifact` | `v3` |
| `actions/deploy-pages` | `v4` |

> **Pre-requisite**: GitHub Pages must be enabled in repo settings with
source set to **GitHub Actions** (`Settings → Pages → Source → GitHub
Actions`).

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

📱 Kick off Copilot coding agent tasks wherever you are with [GitHub
Mobile](https://gh.io/cca-mobile-docs), available on iOS and Android.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: sachitv <1705581+sachitv@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: PRB Generator — robust error display with full Jira response

- Rewrote prbCreate() promise chain: defect and story errors captured independently
  (previously, defect failure would shadow story error or swallow it entirely)
- Added 'Show Jira response' debug collapse alongside payload debug in error display
- New captureError() preserves e.responseData from relay error objects

commit.message:
fix: compute avgRetention as weighted period-level KPI

Change computeAverageRetention to accept the raw data array and compute
totalReturning / totalActive across all days, giving a true period-level
retention rate. The previous unweighted mean of already-rounded daily
percentages could diverge significantly from this KPI on days with very
different traffic volumes. Update callers in CLIAdoptionTrendChart and
AdoptionTrendChart; remove the now-unused retentionRates variable from
AdoptionTrendChart.

commit.message:
refactor: extract detectsEndOfMonthStandardDominance as pure predicate

Decouple scanQuotaExhaustion (flag scanner) from computeBillingCycleInsight
(UI chart insight) by extracting the core billing-cycle detection logic into
a shared pure boolean predicate. Both callers now reference the predicate
directly, so future changes to chart copy or insight shape won't silently
alter flagging behaviour.

commit.message:
feat(tool-guard): add default git-safety and destructive-ops presets

Introduces skills/tool-guard/defaults/ with two composable policy presets:
- git-safety.json: deny force-push/reset-hard/clean-f; warn bare-push/branch-D/checkout/restore
- destructive-ops.json: deny rm -rf variants; warn mv with ../abs-path + PowerShell equivalents

Adds Step 0 to SKILL.md offering these presets before custom input collection.
Expands description with trigger phrases for git guardrail and destructive-delete requests.

Reviewer findings addressed:
- git push --force reason no longer recommends --force-with-lease (also caught by pattern)
- mv destination-position limitation documented in preset _description

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: unify explicit passwordless auth

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: normalize structured LLM content in serialization and memory updater (#1215)

* fix: normalize ToolMessage structured content in serialization

When models return ToolMessage content as a list of content blocks
(e.g. [{"type": "text", "text": "..."}]), the UI previously displayed
the raw Python repr string instead of the extracted text.

Replace str(msg.content) with the existing _extract_text() helper in
both _serialize_message() and stream() to properly normalize
list-of-blocks content to plain text.

Fixes #1149

Also fixes the same root cause as #1188 (characters displayed one per
line when tool response content is returned as structured blocks).

Added 11 regression tests covering string, list-of-blocks, mixed,
empty, and fallback content types.

* fix(memory): extract text from structured LLM responses in memory updater

When LLMs return response content as list of content blocks
(e.g. [{"type": "text", "text": "..."}]) instead of plain strings,
str() produces Python repr which breaks JSON parsing in the memory
updater. This caused memory updates to silently fail.

Changes:
- Add _extract_text() helper in updater.py for safe content normalization
- Use _extract_text() instead of str(response.content) in update_memory()
- Fix format_conversation_for_update() to handle plain strings in list content
- Fix subagent executor fallback path to extract text from list content
- Replace print() with structured logging (logger.info/warning/error)
- Add 13 regression tests covering _extract_text, format_conversation,
  and update_memory with structured LLM responses

* fix: address Copilot review - defensive text extraction + logger.exception

- client.py _extract_text: use block.get('text') + isinstance check (prevent KeyError/TypeError)
- prompt.py format_conversation_for_update: same defensive check for dict text blocks
- executor.py: type-safe text extraction in both code paths, fallback to placeholder instead of str(raw_content)
- updater.py: use logger.exception() instead of logger.error() for traceback preservation

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: preserve chunked structured content without spurious newlines

* fix: restore backend unit test compatibility

---------

Co-authored-by: Exploreunive <Exploreunive@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Willem Jiang <willem.jiang@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(e2e): always render stats page title regardless of loading state (#856)

The CollectionStats component had early returns for loading, error,
and null-stats states that did not include the .stats-page-title
element. In the Docker Compose integration environment, if the
/v1/stats/ API is slow or unavailable, the component stays in a
loading/error state and the E2E navigation test fails because the
selector .stats-page-title is never rendered.

Restructure CollectionStats to always render the page title header
first, then conditionally render loading, error, or data content
below it. This follows the same pattern used by LibraryPage which
always renders .page-title.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: aithena
repository.description: A system to extract knowledge from books

commit.message:
fix(tool-guard): fix emoji corruption in generated PowerShell hooks

ConvertTo-Json on Windows does not escape non-ASCII to \\uXXXX by
default, causing the warning emoji (U+26A0 U+FE0F) to render as
'??' in many console environments.

Changes applied to copilot-cli.md and claude-code.md PS1 templates:

commit.message:
fix(ci): fix failing CI checks on Copilot branch

Root causes of the 2 failed CI checks:
1. Node.js CI used 'npm ci' on a pnpm monorepo — no package-lock.json exists
2. Docker Image CI expected Dockerfile at repo root — it lives at backend/Dockerfile
3. pnpm-lock.yaml was not updated for the new @dnd-kit/* deps added by Copilot
4. -- --runInBand was passed to pnpm test which forwarded it as a Jest pattern arg

Fixes applied:
- Rewrite Node.js CI to use pnpm/action-setup and run per-workspace lint/test/build
- Fix Docker Image CI to reference the real Dockerfile paths (backend/Dockerfile.prod,
  frontend/Dockerfile.prod) and use docker/build-push-action
- Update pnpm-lock.yaml to include @dnd-kit/core, @dnd-kit/sortable, @dnd-kit/utilities,
  and @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu
- Remove '-- --runInBand' from test step (flag was being treated as a path pattern)
- Migrate backend ESLint to flat config (eslint.config.mjs), fix controller test mocks
- Fix frontend tsconfig rootDir/target/skipLibCheck for production build
- Add Copilot instructions, PR template, CODEOWNERS, and issue templates
- Add backend/Dockerfile.prod and confirm frontend/Dockerfile.prod is present

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Lagrange evaluation for Shamir's Secret Sharing in cryptography section

Implement Lagrange interpolation applied to cryptographic secret sharing:
- lagrange_basis: Compute i-th basis polynomial with optional modular arithmetic
- lagrange_interpolate: Evaluate interpolating polynomial over reals or finite fields
- generate_shares: Create Shamir's Secret Sharing shares
- reconstruct_secret: Recover secret from threshold shares via Lagrange at x=0

Includes 30 pytest tests and an example script.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix KV cache context bleed in chat command

mlx_lm.server uses a trie-based prefix cache shared across ALL requests.
Single-turn messages share the '<|im_start|>user\n' token prefix, so a
new chat request could match a cached prefix from an unrelated earlier
request and inherit its context.

Fix: prepend a unique system message to every chat request so the token
sequence prefix is always different and never hits the shared cache.
Only affects 'mlx-server chat' — interactive REPL and direct API calls
are intentionally unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: The open source AI engineering platform for agents, LLMs, and ML models. MLflow enables teams of all sizes to debug, evaluate, monitor, and optimize production-quality AI applications while controlling costs and managing access to models and data.

commit.message:
Add README

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix audit issues: event ordering, thread safety, layout gaps, world hop

H1: Move glass snapshot trigger into SessionTracker's own @Subscribe
    handler so it fires after BankScanner updates (correct event order)
H2: Gate updateComponentTreeUI to first panel activation only
H3: Route reset button through clientThread.invokeLater() to avoid
    cross-thread writes to tracker state
M1: Add setVisible() to collapsible wrappers to eliminate DynamicGridLayout
    double-gap artifacts
M2: Add Math.max(1, ...) guard on glassPerCast to prevent division by zero
    from corrupted config
M3: Preserve bank data on world hop by checking previousGameState != HOPPING

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve Copilot eBPF SSL capture pipeline

- add robust symbol-offset fallback mapping for Copilot ELF uprobes

- increase BPF capture chunk size and fix SSL_read_ex byte-length handling

- add connection rolling buffers and lossy UTF-8 analysis for mixed payloads

- keep Copilot raw/SSL diagnostics in event path for validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Plugin audit fixes: thread safety, cleanup, and quality improvements

- Wrap panel.update() in SwingUtilities.invokeLater (EDT thread safety)
- Filter onStatChanged to Crafting/Magic only (avoid unnecessary updates)
- Make panel 'active' field volatile for cross-thread visibility
- Clamp progress bar to 0-100% range
- Remove unused constants (SODA_ASH, SEAWEED, FIRE_RUNE, AIR_RUNE, BERT_DAILY_SAND, etc.)
- Remove unused sodaAshCount from BankScanner
- Fix description in runelite-plugin.properties (remove nonexistent farming reminders)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Glassblowing overlay, session persistence, and UI state improvements

- Add glassblowing overlay with items blown, items to goal/level, crafting XP
- Add config toggles: items to goal, factor make XP, reset session on logout
- Track inventory materials for accurate casts-left across bank + inventory
- Derive glass produced from total glass delta (no false counts on withdraw)
- Persist session stats across logouts via soft reset (freeze + re-snapshot)
- Show 'Log in to view goal progress' when logged out instead of stale data
- Reset button now clears both trackers and refreshes UI immediately
- Panel updates on logout to reflect reset state

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update dashboard.py
